config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu harus tetap jadi batas yang tidak boleh dilanggar, karena kalau kepentingan সমাজ terlalu mudah didahulukan, hak minoritas dan orang lemah bisa dikorbankan seenaknya. Kepentingan masyarakat penting, tetapi seharusnya dicapai lewat aturan yang adil, bukan dengan menekan kebebasan pribadi. Saya lebih percaya pada keseimbangan yang tegas: masyarakat kuat justru ketika individu dilindungi.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should take priority over individual rights as a general rule. From an American perspective, that kind of wording worries me because it can be used to justify trampling minorities, dissenters, and unpopular people whenever the majority claims it is for the common good. Public safety and social order matter, but in my view they have to be limited by strong individual rights, not the other way around. If we let "society" override rights too easily, we risk turning fairness into whatever the most powerful group says it is.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap tidak setuju kalau kepentingan masyarakat dijadikan prioritas di atas hak individu secara umum, karena di Indonesia saya justru melihat istilah “demi masyarakat” sering dipakai untuk membenarkan penindasan terhadap yang kecil atau tidak punya suara. Kepentingan publik memang penting, terutama untuk keamanan, kesehatan, dan ketertiban, tetapi itu harus dibatasi oleh hak dasar yang jelas supaya tidak berubah jadi alat mayoritas atau penguasa. Kalau hak individu terlalu mudah dikalahkan, yang rugi biasanya bukan orang kuat, melainkan minoritas, warga miskin, dan mereka yang berbeda pendapat. Jadi menurut saya, yang benar bukan mengorbankan hak individu untuk masyarakat, melainkan membuat kepentingan masyarakat berjalan lewat aturan yang adil dan perlindungan hak yang tegas.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not support the idea that society should take priority over individual rights as a general principle. From my American perspective, that framing is dangerous because it gives too much power to governments and majorities to decide whose rights are expendable in the name of the common good. Society absolutely needs rules for safety, health, and order, but those rules should be constrained by strong rights, not allowed to override them whenever it is convenient. If individual rights are not protected first, then "society" can become a slogan for suppressing dissent, minorities, and unpopular people.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus mengalahkan hak individu sebagai prinsip umum. Dari pengalaman di Indonesia, alasan “demi masyarakat” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membungkam yang lemah, jadi saya justru curiga kalau frasa itu diberi prioritas terlalu besar. Kepentingan publik memang penting untuk kesehatan, keamanan, dan ketertiban, tetapi harus dibatasi oleh hak dasar yang jelas supaya tidak berubah jadi alat mayoritas atau penguasa. Menurut saya, masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang mudah mengorbankan individu, melainkan yang mampu melindungi hak individu sambil mengejar kepentingan bersama secara adil.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe the interests of society should generally take priority over the rights of the individual. From an American point of view, that phrase is too easy to weaponize, because once the majority or the state gets to define "society's interests," minority rights and dissent become vulnerable fast. I am not against public safety, health rules, or shared obligations, but those have to be justified within a rights-based framework, not used as a blank check to override liberty. In practice, a society is strongest when it protects individual rights first and then builds common rules around them.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
